page: Microsoft Azure A8_v2 is an Av2-series virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor features a 50 MB L3 cache and supports hardware flags such as AVX, AES cryptography, and FMA. Local storage is limited to an 82 GB temporary disk, and there are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates that the A8_v2 exhibits entry-level performance characteristics. In computational testing, the single-core stress-ng score of 349.68 and multi-core score of 2724.47 fall into the bottom 10% and bottom 25% of tested servers, respectively. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with a read-only large block bandwidth of 26.28 GB/sec and a PassMark memory score of 790.03, placing it in the lower tiers of memory throughput. Cryptographic and compression tasks also reflect these limitations, as shown by a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 9.17 GB/sec and a PassMark encryption test score of 974.2.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation but modest processing and memory bandwidth, the A8_v2 is positioned as a budget-conscious option for workloads that do not require high-performance computing. It is suitable for basic web serving, light database operations, and development or testing environments. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a cost-efficient entry point for legacy x86 applications, though resource-intensive tasks like large-scale data processing or real-time LLM inference are limited by the hardware's performance profile.
description: Microsoft Azure A8_v2 is an Av2-series virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per core, alongside 82 GB of local temporary storage. Lacking GPU acceleration, this x86_64 server is designed for entry-level workloads. Benchmark results place its compute and memory performance in the lower tiers, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 2724.47 and a PassMark memory score of 790.03. Consequently, the A8_v2 is best suited for light database operations, basic web serving, and development environments where cost efficiency is prioritized over high-throughput processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure A8_v2 is an Av2-series VM with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 82 GB local storage, offering entry-level performance for basic web serving and development workloads.

tagline: An entry-level, budget-friendly virtual machine with dedicated Intel processors and local storage for basic development and light web serving workloads.
